---
title: "Analysis of Cars"
author: Kelun Wang
date: today
description: "A short exploratory analysis of the relationship between fuel efficiency and engine displacement in the mtcars dataset."
---

This mini project explores the relationship between fuel efficiency (`mpg`) and engine displacement (`disp`) in the `mtcars` dataset. The two variables have a correlation of `r cor(mtcars$mpg, mtcars$disp) |> format(digits=2)`, which suggests a strong negative association.

## Scatterplot

```{r}
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)
data(mtcars)
mtcars |>
  ggplot(aes(mpg, disp)) +
  geom_point(color="dodgerblue4", size=2) +
  labs(
    title = "Fuel Efficiency and Engine Displacement Move in Opposite Directions",
    x = "Miles per gallon",
    y = "Engine displacement"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

Cars with larger engines tend to have lower fuel efficiency, which is exactly the pattern we would expect from the plot. This page is intentionally simple, but it serves as a first example of how I want to present analysis on the site.
